hx_brief: 62yo male with severe chest pain that started suddenly while lifting groceries. He says it feels different from anything he's had before.

hints.hx: He describes it as a deep, intense pain that seems to move. Has been on no medications despite knowing his blood pressure runs high for years.
hints.pe: You notice his blood pressure readings are oddly different between his arms. A faint murmur is audible at the aortic area.
hints.img: CTA Chest: A linear density is visualized within the aortic lumen separating two distinct flow channels.

lab_results.EKG: Left axis deviation, no ST changes
lab_results.Specialty: Markedly elevated at 3,500 ng/mL — nonspecific, does not localize origin.

hx_brief: 24yo female with 2 days of abdominal pain and one episode of vomiting. She hasn't had much of an appetite since it started.

hints.hx: The pain started near her belly button and has since shifted. She rates it 7/10 and says movement makes it worse. No recent travel or sick contacts.
hints.pe: Tenderness localizes to the right lower quadrant. Palpating the left lower quadrant reproduces her pain on the right. Passive extension of the right hip worsens it.
hints.img: CT Abdomen/Pelvis: A blind-ending tubular structure in the RLQ measuring 8.5mm in diameter with surrounding inflammatory fat stranding.

lab_results.UA: Few WBCs, no nitrites, no casts
lab_results.Specialty: CRP 42 mg/L (elevated).

hx_brief: 55yo female with a severe headache and blurry vision that came on suddenly an hour ago. She feels nauseated and the light is bothering her.

hints.hx: She says the pain is worst around her right eye. She noticed colored rings around lights earlier today before the headache started. No prior headache history.
hints.pe: Right eye appears hazy on inspection. The pupil on that side is mid-dilated and does not react to light. The cornea has a ground-glass appearance.
hints.img: Slit-lamp exam: Anterior chamber is shallow. Gonioscopy confirms complete closure of the iridocorneal drainage angle.

lab_results.Specialty: IOP right eye: 61 mmHg. Left eye: 14 mmHg. (Normal: 10–21 mmHg)

hx_brief: 32yo male brought in by his roommate for progressive difficulty walking over the past 3 days. He keeps saying his legs feel heavy.

hints.hx: He had a GI illness about 2 weeks ago with crampy diarrhea that resolved on its own. The weakness started in his feet and has been creeping upward since.
hints.pe: Bilateral lower extremity weakness, worse distally. Deep tendon reflexes are absent at the knees and ankles. Sensation is mildly reduced in a stocking distribution.
hints.img: MRI Spine: No cord compression or intrinsic cord signal change. Subtle enhancement of the ventral nerve roots of the cauda equina.

lab_results.ABG: NIF (Negative Inspiratory Force) reduced at -22 cmH2O
lab_results.Specialty: CSF: Protein 310 mg/dL (high), WBC 3 cells/µL (normal). Classic albuminocytologic dissociation.

hx_brief: 68yo male noticed blood in his urine on and off for the past month. He has no pain and feels otherwise well.

hints.hx: He smoked a pack a day for 30 years, quit 10 years ago. He worked in industrial manufacturing for most of his career. No UTI symptoms, no flank pain.
hints.pe: Abdomen is soft and non-tender. No costovertebral angle tenderness. DRE is normal. No palpable lymphadenopathy.
hints.img: Cystoscopy: Multiple raised, irregular lesions visualized near the trigone. Lesions have a papillary, frond-like architecture.

lab_results.UA: 3+ blood, no casts, no dysmorphic RBCs, no nitrites
lab_results.Specialty: Atypical urothelial cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio — high-grade urothelial carcinoma.

hx_brief: 45yo obese female with recurring episodes of right-sided abdominal pain. Today's episode is the worst one yet and she's been vomiting.

hints.hx: The pain typically comes on about 30–40 minutes after eating and lasts a few hours before resolving. Today it hasn't gone away. She also feels feverish.
hints.pe: She winces and stops inhaling when you deeply palpate the right upper quadrant. Mild jaundice is not present. Low-grade fever at 38.2°C.
hints.img: HIDA scan: Tracer is taken up by the liver normally but the gallbladder is never visualized even on delayed imaging.

lab_results.Specialty: AST 52, ALT 61 (mildly elevated). Total bilirubin normal. ALP mildly elevated.

hx_brief: 19yo male athlete is brought to the ED after collapsing on the court during a basketball game. Bystander CPR was initiated. He did not survive.

hints.hx: His parents state he had no known medical conditions, though he occasionally complained of feeling lightheaded during intense exercise. His father passed away unexpectedly at 32.
hints.pe: On prior sports physical (chart review): A harsh systolic murmur was noted at the left sternal border. It was documented as louder when standing and softer when squatting.
hints.img: Echocardiogram (post-mortem review): Asymmetric septal hypertrophy with a septal-to-posterior wall ratio >1.5. Systolic anterior motion of the mitral valve leaflet observed.

lab_results.EKG: Deep narrow Q waves in leads I, aVL, and V5–V6. Increased voltage.
lab_results.Trop: Elevated — consistent with demand ischemia peri-arrest
lab_results.Specialty: Pathogenic variant identified in MYH7 gene encoding beta-myosin heavy chain.

hx_brief: 28yo female with a 3-month history of progressive exertional dyspnea and Raynaud phenomenon.

hints.hx: No history of smoking or asthma. Recent onset of tight, shiny skin over her fingers (sclerodactyly).
hints.pe: Lungs are clear to auscultation. Precordial heave and a loud S2 (pulmonic component) at the left upper sternal border.
hints.img: Right Heart Cath: Mean Pulmonary Artery Pressure (mPAP) is 30 mmHg at rest (Normal <20).

lab_results.Specialty: Positive Anti-centromere antibodies (1:640 titer).

hx_brief: 42yo male with 2 weeks of worsening thirst, frequent urination, and deep, aching bone pain.

hints.hx: Patient has a history of neck surgery 5 years ago for a thyroid nodule. Constipation and abdominal moans.
hints.pe: Decreased deep tendon reflexes. Mild muscle weakness. No palpable neck masses.
hints.img: X-ray: Subperiosteal bone resorption in the phalanges; 'Salt and pepper' skull.

lab_results.Specialty: Parathyroid Hormone (PTH) is 450 pg/mL (Severely High).

hx_brief: 52yo male with 4 months of cough that won't go away and one episode of coughing up blood last week.

hints.hx: 30 pack-year smoking history. He's noticed a new high-pitched wheeze when breathing. His right arm has been swelling more than the left over the past few weeks.
hints.pe: Dullness to percussion over the right upper lobe. Decreased breath sounds on the right. Prominent neck veins on the right side.
hints.img: CT Chest with contrast: A spiculated central mass in the right hilum causing luminal narrowing of the right mainstem bronchus. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy present.

lab_results.Specialty: Histopathology: Polygonal cells with intercellular bridges and keratin pearl formation.

hx_brief: 31yo female with acute onset of severe abdominal pain and confusion that started last night after a social event where she had a few drinks.

hints.hx: She has had 3 similar episodes of abdominal pain in the past 2 years — each time workup was unremarkable. She also noticed her urine looked darker than usual this morning.
hints.pe: Despite reporting 10/10 abdominal pain, her abdomen is completely soft with no guarding, rigidity, or rebound tenderness. Mild tachycardia.
hints.img: CT Abdomen/Pelvis: No acute intra-abdominal pathology. No free air, no obstruction, no appendiceal or gallbladder abnormality.

lab_results.UA: No blood on dipstick; sample left in light turns dark purple
lab_results.Specialty: Urine porphobilinogen (PBG): markedly elevated. Watson-Schwartz test positive.

hx_brief: 65yo male with 3 days of worsening back pain who now can't control his bladder. His wife says he's been tripping over his own feet.

hints.hx: He was diagnosed with prostate cancer 2 years ago and completed radiation therapy. He describes shooting electric pains down both legs that started before the back pain.
hints.pe: Point tenderness over the lower thoracic spine. Bilateral lower extremity hyperreflexia. Reduced anal sphincter tone on rectal exam.
hints.img: MRI Spine with contrast: Epidural soft tissue mass at T11 causing significant cord compression. Marrow replacement signal in multiple vertebral bodies.

lab_results.Specialty: PSA: 85 ng/mL (severely elevated).

hx_brief: 14yo male brought in by his parents with 2 days of fever, headache, and a rash that started on his wrists.

hints.hx: The family returned from a camping trip in rural North Carolina 8 days ago. The rash has been spreading and some spots look darker than others. He has no neck stiffness.
hints.pe: Maculopapular rash present on wrists, ankles, and palms — some lesions are becoming petechial. Temperature 39.4°C. No meningismus.
hints.img: CT Head: No hemorrhage, no mass, no edema. CXR: Clear lung fields bilaterally.

lab_results.CBC: Platelets 88k (low), WBC mildly low, hyponatremia Na 129
lab_results.Specialty: IFA for Rickettsia rickettsii: pending (do not wait for results before treating).

hx_brief: 38yo female presenting with 2 weeks of profound fatigue, easy bruising, and pain throughout her body. Her gums have been bleeding when she brushes her teeth.

hints.hx: She has felt unusually cold and short of breath climbing stairs. No recent infections, no travel, no family history of blood disorders. She noticed her gums look swollen.
hints.pe: Conjunctival pallor. Scattered petechiae on the trunk and lower extremities. Gingival hyperplasia noted on oral exam. No hepatosplenomegaly appreciated.
hints.img: Bone marrow biopsy: Hypercellular marrow with sheets of immature blasts comprising >20% of nucleated cells. Normal architecture is effaced.

lab_results.Coags: PT/PTT prolonged, fibrinogen low — consistent with DIC
lab_results.Specialty: MPO stain: strongly positive. Auer rods identified on peripheral smear.

hx_brief: 17yo male brought in by his mother after she noticed his eyes looked yellow. He's been tired for weeks and hasn't been eating much.

hints.hx: He recently returned from a semester abroad in Southeast Asia. Denies IV drug use. No prior liver issues. Ate a lot of street food during his trip.
hints.pe: Scleral icterus. Mild right upper quadrant tenderness. No splenomegaly. Low-grade fever at 38.0°C.
hints.img: RUQ Ultrasound: No biliary dilation. Liver is mildly enlarged with diffusely increased echogenicity. No focal lesions.

lab_results.CBC: WBC 4.2 with atypical lymphocytes, mild thrombocytopenia
lab_results.CMP: ALT 820, AST 640 (markedly elevated). Total bilirubin 4.8. Albumin normal.
lab_results.Specialty: IgM anti-HAV: Positive. IgM anti-HBc: Negative. HBsAg: Negative.

hx_brief: 44yo female with 3 weeks of progressively worsening fatigue, weight gain, and feeling cold all the time. Her husband says she seems more forgetful than usual.

hints.hx: She had radioactive iodine treatment for hyperthyroidism 8 months ago. Her periods have become heavier and irregular. She denies depression but her affect is noticeably flat.
hints.pe: Puffy face and periorbital edema. Skin is dry and cool. Diffuse hair thinning. Delayed relaxation phase of deep tendon reflexes bilaterally. HR 52.
hints.img: CXR: Mildly enlarged cardiac silhouette. Echocardiogram: Small pericardial effusion.

lab_results.CMP: Elevated LDL and total cholesterol. Na 132 (mild hyponatremia). CK mildly elevated.
lab_results.Specialty: TSH: 88 mIU/L (severely elevated). Free T4: 0.4 ng/dL (low). Anti-TPO antibodies: Positive.

hx_brief: 29yo male with episodic severe headaches, sweating, and palpitations. He says the attacks last 15-20 minutes and leave him feeling drained.

hints.hx: Episodes are triggered by stress, exercise, and certain foods. His BP during an episode was recorded at 210/130. He has a family history of endocrine tumors.
hints.pe: BP today is 148/92. Mild diaphoresis. No thyroid enlargement. Abdomen soft — no palpable mass.
hints.img: MRI Abdomen: 4.2cm heterogeneous mass arising from the right adrenal gland with central necrosis.

lab_results.EKG: Sinus tachycardia during symptomatic period. Normal at baseline.
lab_results.Specialty: Urinary metanephrines: 4.8x upper limit of normal. Urinary catecholamines: Markedly elevated.

hx_brief: 67yo male with 6 months of progressive difficulty swallowing. It started with solids and has now progressed to liquids. He has lost 18 pounds.

hints.hx: 40 pack-year smoking history. Drinks 3-4 whiskeys nightly for 30 years. He describes food getting stuck mid-chest. No heartburn. No family history of GI cancer.
hints.pe: Cachectic appearance. Supraclavicular lymph node palpable on the left. No abdominal masses. Mild hoarseness noted.
hints.img: CT Chest/Abdomen: Irregular circumferential thickening of the mid-esophagus. Multiple mediastinal lymph nodes enlarged.

lab_results.Specialty: Biopsy: Poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the mid-esophagus.

hx_brief: 52yo female with sudden onset of the worst headache of her life. She was watching TV when it hit and describes it as an explosion in her head.

hints.hx: No prior headache history. She vomited once immediately after onset. Briefly lost consciousness. No fever. Neck stiffness developed within an hour.
hints.pe: Alert but distressed. Nuchal rigidity present. Photophobia. No focal neurological deficits. Papilledema on fundoscopy.
hints.img: CT Head non-contrast: Hyperdense blood in the basal cisterns and sylvian fissures bilaterally.

lab_results.EKG: Diffuse T-wave inversions and QT prolongation
lab_results.Specialty: CSF: Xanthochromia present. RBCs that do not clear across tubes. Opening pressure 28 cmH2O.

hx_brief: 35yo female with 2 months of joint pain, a facial rash that gets worse in the sun, and persistent fatigue.

hints.hx: The rash appeared after a beach vacation. She also reports oral ulcers, hair thinning, and occasional chest pain when taking a deep breath.
hints.pe: Erythematous rash across both cheeks sparing the nasolabial folds. Painless oral ulcer. Mild bilateral wrist and MCP joint swelling.
hints.img: CXR: Small bilateral pleural effusions. Echocardiogram: Trace pericardial effusion.

lab_results.CBC: Leukopenia WBC 3.1. Mild thrombocytopenia Plts 98k. Hgb 10.4.
lab_results.Specialty: ANA: Positive 1:640 homogeneous. Anti-dsDNA: Positive. Anti-Smith: Positive. Complement C3/C4: Low.

hx_brief: 58yo male with 4 months of progressive exertional chest tightness that resolves with rest. Today it came on while walking to his car.

hints.hx: Hypertension and type 2 diabetes for 10 years, poorly controlled. 25 pack-year smoker, quit 3 years ago. Father had a heart attack at 55. Pain radiates to his left arm.
hints.pe: BP 158/94. Mild S4 gallop. No murmurs. No peripheral edema. Pulses intact bilaterally.
hints.img: Nuclear stress test: Fixed perfusion defect in the LAD territory. No reversible ischemia.

lab_results.EKG: ST depression in V4-V6 during chest pain that resolves at rest.
lab_results.Specialty: Angiography: 75% stenosis of proximal LAD. 60% stenosis of RCA.

hx_brief: 71yo male with 2 days of confusion and decreased urine output. His wife says he was fine until he started a new medication last week.

hints.hx: Started an NSAID for back pain 8 days ago. History of CHF and CKD stage 2 at baseline. Also takes an ACE inhibitor and furosemide. No fevers, no dysuria.
hints.pe: BP 88/60. HR 112. Dry mucous membranes. No rales. JVP flat. Skin turgor decreased.
hints.img: Renal Ultrasound: Kidneys normal size bilaterally, no hydronephrosis.

lab_results.EKG: Peaked T waves in precordial leads
lab_results.Specialty: Urine Na: 8 mEq/L. FeNa: 0.4% — prerenal pattern.

hx_brief: 23yo female with 3 days of burning with urination, frequency, and lower abdominal discomfort. She is otherwise healthy.

hints.hx: Sexually active with one partner. Uses oral contraceptives. No vaginal discharge. No fever or flank pain.
hints.pe: Mild suprapubic tenderness. No CVA tenderness. Normal pelvic exam. No cervical motion tenderness.
hints.img: Renal Ultrasound: Normal. No hydronephrosis.

lab_results.UA: Positive nitrites, 3+ leukocyte esterase, many WBCs, few RBCs
lab_results.Specialty: Culture: >100,000 CFU/mL E. coli — sensitive to TMP-SMX.

hx_brief: 48yo male presents with several months of gradually worsening back pain, fatigue, and his wife mentions he seems more confused lately. He fractured his wrist last month stepping off a curb.

hints.hx: No history of osteoporosis and the fracture seemed disproportionate to the mechanism. Reports constant thirst and urinating more. Denies weight loss.
hints.pe: Pale conjunctiva. Mild bony tenderness along the spine. No focal neurologic deficits. No lymphadenopathy.
hints.img: Skeletal survey: Multiple lytic punched-out lesions in the skull and vertebrae. No sclerotic lesions.

lab_results.CBC: Hgb 8.2 — normocytic anemia. Rouleaux formation on smear.
lab_results.CMP: Calcium 12.4 (high). Creatinine 2.1. Total protein markedly elevated.
lab_results.UA: Protein 2+ — Bence Jones proteins on UPEP
lab_results.Specialty: SPEP: Monoclonal IgG kappa spike. Bone marrow: 35% plasma cells.

hx_brief: 34yo female presents with 6 weeks of muscle weakness that is worse at the end of the day. She has been having difficulty chewing through meals and her eyelids droop by evening.

hints.hx: Symptoms improve dramatically in the morning and after rest. She noticed her speech becomes slurred after talking for a while. No pain. No rash.
hints.pe: Bilateral ptosis that worsens with sustained upward gaze. Fatigable proximal limb weakness. Normal sensation. Ice pack test: Ptosis improves after 2 minutes.
hints.img: CT Chest: 3.1cm anterior mediastinal mass — thymoma suspected.

lab_results.Specialty: Anti-AChR antibodies: Positive (elevated titer). Anti-MuSK: Negative.

hx_brief: 61yo male with sudden onset right-sided weakness and slurred speech that started 45 minutes ago. His wife called 911 immediately.

hints.hx: History of atrial fibrillation, not on anticoagulation. HTN and hyperlipidemia. No prior strokes. Symptoms started acutely.
hints.pe: BP 188/102. Right facial droop. Right arm strength 1/5, right leg 2/5. Expressive aphasia. NIHSS score 16.
hints.img: CT Head: No hemorrhage. CT Angiography: Occlusion of the left MCA M1 segment.

lab_results.EKG: Atrial fibrillation with controlled ventricular rate
lab_results.Specialty: TEE: Thrombus in left atrial appendage — cardioembolic source confirmed.

hx_brief: 26yo male presents with 2 weeks of swollen lymph nodes in his neck and armpit, drenching night sweats, and an unintentional 12-pound weight loss.

hints.hx: No recent infections. Non-smoker, social drinker. Intense itching all over his body without a rash. Intermittent fevers at home.
hints.pe: Firm, rubbery, non-tender lymphadenopathy in cervical, axillary, and inguinal regions. Spleen tip palpable.
hints.img: CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis: Mediastinal and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. Splenomegaly.

lab_results.Specialty: Excisional biopsy: Reed-Sternberg cells — large binucleated cells with owl-eye nucleoli. CD15+, CD30+.

hx_brief: 72yo female brought in by her son for progressive memory loss over the past 2 years. She got lost driving to the grocery store she has visited for 20 years.

hints.hx: Her son reports she repeats herself constantly. She has been forgetting names of close family members. She has become increasingly paranoid and accuses family of stealing.
hints.pe: MMSE 17/30 — impaired recall, orientation, and language. No focal deficits. No tremor. No gait abnormality.
hints.img: MRI Brain: Diffuse cortical atrophy most prominent in bilateral hippocampi and temporal lobes. No focal lesions.

lab_results.Specialty: Amyloid PET: Diffuse amyloid deposition in frontal and temporal cortices. CSF: Low Abeta-42, elevated tau.

hx_brief: 19yo female brought to the ED by her roommate after she became confused and started seizing in their dorm room. She had a headache and stiff neck for 2 days.

hints.hx: She just started college and lives in a dormitory. No prior seizure history. Not vaccinated against meningococcus. Denies drug use.
hints.pe: Temperature 39.8°C. HR 124. Kernig and Brudzinski signs positive. Non-blanching petechial rash on trunk and lower extremities.
hints.img: CT Head: No mass lesion, no herniation — cleared for LP.

lab_results.Specialty: CSF: Cloudy. WBC 4,200 neutrophilic. Protein 280. Glucose 22. Gram stain: Gram-negative diplococci.

hx_brief: 55yo male with 3 months of progressive shortness of breath and bilateral leg swelling. He can no longer climb a single flight of stairs without stopping.

hints.hx: History of two prior heart attacks, the last 4 years ago. Sleeps on 3 pillows and wakes up gasping at night. Denies chest pain currently.
hints.pe: BP 102/68. HR 98. JVP elevated at 12cm. Bilateral crackles at lung bases. S3 gallop. 3+ pitting edema to the thighs.
hints.img: Echocardiogram: EF 22%. Globally dilated LV. Moderate mitral regurgitation. Moderate pulmonary hypertension.

lab_results.Specialty: BNP: 2,840 pg/mL.

hx_brief: 41yo male with sudden onset right flank pain that radiates to the groin. The pain is colicky and he cannot find a comfortable position.

hints.hx: No fever. Had one similar episode 2 years ago that resolved on its own. Nausea but no vomiting. Drinks very little water daily.
hints.pe: Right CVA tenderness. Abdomen soft and non-tender. Patient is writhing in pain.
hints.img: CT Abdomen/Pelvis non-contrast: 6mm hyperdense calculus in the right distal ureter at the UVJ. Mild right hydronephrosis.

lab_results.Specialty: Stone analysis: Calcium oxalate monohydrate (80%). Urine supersaturation for oxalate elevated.

hx_brief: 33yo female with 4 months of episodic flushing, diarrhea, and wheezing. The episodes seem to come on randomly and last about 20 minutes.

hints.hx: Episodes triggered by alcohol and stress. She has lost 8 pounds without trying. Primary care thought it was anxiety but medications haven't helped.
hints.pe: BP 148/88 during a flushing episode. Facial flushing present. Hepatomegaly on exam.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Multiple hepatic lesions. Primary mass in the distal ileum — 2.8cm with mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

lab_results.Specialty: 24hr urine 5-HIAA: 88 mg/24hr (normal <8). Chromogranin A: Elevated.

hx_brief: 25yo male with acute onset of severe left testicular pain that woke him from sleep 3 hours ago. The pain is constant and he vomited twice.

hints.hx: No trauma. No urinary symptoms. No fever. No prior episodes. Pain has not improved with ibuprofen.
hints.pe: Left testicle is high-riding and lies in a horizontal orientation. Exquisitely tender. Cremasteric reflex absent on the left. Scrotal erythema developing.
hints.img: Scrotal Doppler Ultrasound: Absent blood flow to the left testicle. Normal flow to the right.

lab_results.Specialty: Intraoperative: 720-degree torsion of left spermatic cord. Testicle non-viable — orchiectomy performed.

hx_brief: 47yo female with recurrent episodes of abdominal pain, diarrhea, and skin rashes on her elbows and knees for years. She thought she had IBS.

hints.hx: Iron deficiency anemia that has never responded to oral iron supplements. Bloating after bread and pasta. Her sister has the same condition.
hints.pe: Pale. BMI 17.4. Oral aphthous ulcers. Abdomen distended with mild diffuse tenderness.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Diffuse small bowel wall thickening with mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

lab_results.Coags: PT mildly prolonged — vitamin K malabsorption
lab_results.Specialty: Anti-tTG IgA: Strongly positive. Duodenal biopsy: Villous atrophy and crypt hyperplasia — Marsh grade 3c.

hx_brief: 39yo male with 2 days of severe right eye pain, blurry vision, and redness. He wears contact lenses and sometimes sleeps in them.

hints.hx: Has been wearing the same pair of contacts for 3 weeks without removing them. No prior eye problems. No systemic symptoms. Pain worsens with light exposure.
hints.pe: Right eye: Marked conjunctival injection and ciliary flush. Fluorescein staining reveals a large central corneal ulcer. Hypopyon visible in the anterior chamber.
hints.img: Slit-lamp: Dense corneal infiltrate with overlying epithelial defect. Anterior chamber reaction 3+.

lab_results.Specialty: Corneal culture: Pseudomonas aeruginosa — sensitive to fluoroquinolones.

hx_brief: 66yo male with 3 months of fatigue, early satiety, and a dragging sensation in his left abdomen. His wife noticed his abdomen looks bigger on the left side.

hints.hx: Reports drenching night sweats and has lost 14 pounds. No lymph node swelling. WBC was elevated on routine labs 6 months ago but he never followed up.
hints.pe: Massive splenomegaly — palpable 14cm below the left costal margin. Mild hepatomegaly. No peripheral lymphadenopathy.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Massive splenomegaly 26cm. Mild hepatomegaly. No significant lymphadenopathy.

lab_results.CBC: WBC 186,000 with full myeloid spectrum — basophilia, myelocytes. Hgb 9.2. Plts 620,000.
lab_results.Specialty: BCR-ABL1: Positive. Cytogenetics: t(9;22)(q34;q11) — Philadelphia chromosome.

hx_brief: 31yo female with 3 weeks of progressive shortness of breath, bilateral leg swelling, and fatigue. She gave birth 5 weeks ago.

hints.hx: Uncomplicated vaginal delivery at 39 weeks. No prior cardiac history. No hypertension during pregnancy. Unable to lie flat since 2 weeks postpartum.
hints.pe: HR 108. BP 96/62. JVP elevated. S3 gallop. Bilateral crackles. Bilateral pitting edema to the knees.
hints.img: Echocardiogram: EF 28%, globally dilated cardiomyopathy. Moderate mitral regurgitation.

lab_results.Specialty: BNP: 3,200 pg/mL. Cardiac MRI: Diffuse myocardial edema — inflammatory pattern.

hx_brief: 77yo male with 3 days of confusion, fever, and decreased urine output. His daughter found him disoriented at home.

hints.hx: History of BPH with poor urinary stream for years. Recently started a new antihistamine. No prior UTIs. Lives alone.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.9°C. BP 88/58. Confused. Suprapubic fullness and tenderness. CVA tenderness on the right.
hints.img: Renal Ultrasound: Bilateral hydronephrosis. Bladder markedly distended at 800mL.

lab_results.Specialty: Urine culture: >100,000 CFU/mL E. coli — ESBL producing.

hx_brief: 52yo male with sudden onset of severe mid-epigastric pain radiating to his back. He rates it 9/10 and says leaning forward helps slightly.

hints.hx: Attended a wedding two days ago and drank heavily. History of hypertriglyceridemia, never treated. Vomiting x6 since onset.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.2°C. HR 118. BP 100/72. Diffuse epigastric tenderness with guarding. Grey Turner sign developing on flanks.
hints.img: CT Abdomen with contrast: Pancreatic edema with peripancreatic fat stranding and fluid collections. No organized necrosis.

lab_results.Specialty: Repeat lipase at 24hrs: 6,200 U/L. Triglycerides: 1,840 mg/dL — hypertriglyceridemia-induced.

hx_brief: 44yo male with 6 weeks of progressively worsening shortness of breath, dry cough, and fatigue. He recently visited his doctor and was told it was asthma.

hints.hx: Non-smoker. Works as an HVAC technician. No asthma medications have helped. He also mentions blurry vision and skin bumps on his shins.
hints.pe: Low-grade fever 37.8°C. Bilateral fine crackles. Tender raised erythematous nodules on anterior shins. Bilateral anterior uveitis. Bilateral hilar dullness.
hints.img: CT Chest: Bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy and diffuse pulmonary infiltrates in a perilymphatic distribution.

lab_results.Specialty: BAL: CD4:CD8 ratio >3.5. Biopsy: Non-caseating granulomas without necrosis.

hx_brief: 28yo male with 4 days of fever, severe sore throat, and difficulty opening his mouth fully. His voice sounds like he has a hot potato in his mouth.

hints.hx: He had a sore throat 10 days ago and took leftover antibiotics for 3 days. Now has worsening throat pain, drooling, and can barely swallow liquids.
hints.pe: Temperature 39.1°C. Muffled voice. Uvula deviated to the right. Bulging of the left peritonsillar area. Left tonsil displaced medially. Trismus present.
hints.img: CT Neck with contrast: 2.8cm rim-enhancing fluid collection in the left peritonsillar space compressing the airway.

lab_results.Specialty: Culture from abscess drainage: Group A Streptococcus and oral anaerobes.

hx_brief: 36yo female with 8 months of joint pain and stiffness that is worst in the morning and takes over an hour to loosen up. Her hands and wrists are swollen symmetrically.

hints.hx: The stiffness improves as the day goes on. She also reports fatigue and a mild rash on her arms. No photosensitivity. Her mother has a similar condition.
hints.pe: Symmetric swelling and tenderness of MCPs, PIPs, and wrists bilaterally. Ulnar deviation of fingers early. No DIP involvement. Rheumatoid nodules on extensor surfaces of elbows.
hints.img: X-rays hands: Periarticular osteopenia and marginal erosions at MCP joints bilaterally.

lab_results.Specialty: Anti-CCP: 280 U/mL (strongly positive). RF: 180 IU/mL. CRP: 48 mg/L. ESR: 78.

hx_brief: 58yo male with sudden onset left-sided weakness and facial drooping that resolved completely after 25 minutes. He is now back to normal in the ED.

hints.hx: History of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. Smokes half a pack daily. No prior neurological events. Symptoms resolved completely before arrival.
hints.pe: BP 172/96. Completely neurologically intact on arrival. Carotid bruit heard on the right.
hints.img: MRI Brain DWI: No acute infarct. MRA Neck: 75% stenosis of the right internal carotid artery.

lab_results.Specialty: Holter Monitor x24hr: No paroxysmal AF detected.

hx_brief: 45yo male with 2 months of progressive lower back pain and new onset bilateral leg weakness. He is a known IV drug user.

hints.hx: Injecting heroin for 10 years. Had a dental procedure 3 months ago. Fevers and chills for 6 weeks. Back pain is constant and worse at night. Now struggles to walk.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.7°C. Point tenderness over L3-L4. Lower extremity weakness 3/5 bilaterally. Hyperreflexia and upgoing plantar response.
hints.img: MRI Spine with contrast: Rim-enhancing epidural collection at L3-L4 compressing the thecal sac. Vertebral endplate erosion.

lab_results.Specialty: Blood cultures x2: MSSA. Echo: 1.2cm vegetation on tricuspid valve.

hx_brief: 19yo female brought in by her roommate after she fainted twice today. She has been restricting her eating for 6 months and exercises 3 hours daily.

hints.hx: She denies there is a problem. Has lost 28 pounds in 6 months. Her periods stopped 4 months ago. She feels cold all the time and has been growing fine hair on her arms.
hints.pe: BMI 14.8. HR 42. BP 84/56. Lanugo on arms and back. Bilateral parotid enlargement. Dry skin. Peripheral edema.
hints.img: Echocardiogram: Mild pericardial effusion. EF preserved.

lab_results.CMP: Na 132. K 3.1. Glucose 58. Phosphorus low. Albumin 2.8.
lab_results.Specialty: DEXA: T-score -2.8 — osteoporosis. FSH/LH suppressed. Estradiol undetectable.

hx_brief: 63yo male with 3 weeks of progressively worsening confusion, shuffling gait, and urinary incontinence. His wife says he used to be sharp and active.

hints.hx: He had a subarachnoid hemorrhage 4 months ago that was surgically managed. No prior dementia. He walks with very small steps and has fallen twice.
hints.pe: Alert but disoriented to date. Magnetic gait — feet appear stuck to floor. Wide-based. Frontal lobe release signs present.
hints.img: MRI Brain: Markedly enlarged ventricles disproportionate to cortical atrophy. Periventricular T2 signal — transependymal CSF flow.

lab_results.Specialty: LP: Opening pressure 22 cmH2O. After removal of 30mL CSF — gait improves significantly within 1 hour.

hx_brief: 42yo female presents with 3 months of muscle weakness, difficulty rising from chairs, and a violaceous rash on her eyelids. Her hands have been rough and cracked.

hints.hx: She noticed the rash before the weakness started. She works as a landscaper. The weakness is worse proximally. No joint pain. No sun sensitivity.
hints.pe: Heliotrope rash on eyelids. Gottron papules over MCP and PIP joints. Proximal muscle strength 2/5 upper and lower extremities. Shawl sign.
hints.img: MRI Thighs: Diffuse muscle edema and inflammation in proximal muscle groups bilaterally.

lab_results.Specialty: CK: 8,400 U/L. Anti-Jo-1: Positive. EMG: Myopathic pattern. Muscle biopsy: Perifascicular atrophy.

hx_brief: 55yo male with 6 hours of severe central chest pain with radiation to his jaw. He is diaphoretic and pale.

hints.hx: No prior cardiac history. Hypertension on amlodipine. The pain started at rest and has not changed with position or breathing. His brother had a heart attack at 48.
hints.pe: BP 92/64. HR 108. Diaphoretic. S4 gallop. No murmur. JVP not elevated. Clear lung fields.
hints.img: Urgent coronary angiography: 100% occlusion of the proximal left anterior descending artery — TIMI 0 flow.

lab_results.EKG: ST elevation in V1-V5 with reciprocal ST depression in II, III, aVF
lab_results.Trop: Initial troponin I 8.4 ng/mL, rising to 42 ng/mL at 3 hours
lab_results.Specialty: Post-PCI: TIMI 3 flow restored. EF 35% post-infarct.

hx_brief: 38yo male with a 2-month history of recurrent bloody diarrhea, urgency, and crampy lower abdominal pain. He has had 8-10 bloody stools per day.

hints.hx: No recent travel, no antibiotics, no sick contacts. He has lost 11 pounds. He has been having joint pain in his knees and ankles. His eyes have been red and irritated.
hints.pe: Pale and uncomfortable. Mild tachycardia. Diffuse lower abdominal tenderness — worst in the left lower quadrant. No peritoneal signs. Oral aphthous ulcers.
hints.img: CT Abdomen/Pelvis: Continuous colonic wall thickening from rectum to splenic flexure. No skip lesions. No free air.

lab_results.Specialty: Colonoscopy: Continuous mucosal inflammation from rectum to splenic flexure. Crypt abscesses on biopsy. No granulomas.

hx_brief: 27yo male with 3 weeks of fever, night sweats, and a painful swollen right knee. He is sexually active with multiple partners.

hints.hx: He denies genital discharge but admits to unprotected sex 3 weeks ago. No prior joint problems. The knee became swollen over 2 days. He also has small painful pustular lesions on his palms.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.4°C. Right knee markedly swollen, warm, and tender — range of motion severely limited. Pustular skin lesions on palms and soles. No urethral discharge today.
hints.img: X-ray right knee: Soft tissue swelling only. No bony erosions. MRI: Large joint effusion with synovial thickening.

lab_results.Specialty: Joint fluid: WBC 68,000 (purulent). Culture: Neisseria gonorrhoeae. Urine NAAT: Positive for gonorrhea.

hx_brief: 70yo male with progressive resting tremor in his right hand for 18 months. His wife says he moves much slower than he used to and his handwriting has become tiny.

hints.hx: The tremor is worse at rest and improves with intentional movement. He has difficulty initiating steps. He sometimes feels like his feet are glued to the floor. No family history. Non-smoker.
hints.pe: Masked facies. Monotone voice. Cogwheel rigidity in bilateral upper extremities. Resting pill-rolling tremor right > left. Bradykinesia. Positive pull test — retropulsion.
hints.img: MRI Brain: No focal lesions. Mild generalized atrophy appropriate for age. DaTscan: Reduced dopamine transporter uptake in bilateral putamen.

lab_results.Specialty: DaTscan SPECT: Asymmetric reduced uptake in bilateral striatum — right > left. Consistent with nigrostriatal degeneration.

hx_brief: 33yo female with 2 years of intermittent right upper quadrant pain, fatigue, and jaundice. Her bilirubin is always elevated on routine labs.

hints.hx: She has no history of alcohol use, no hepatitis exposures, no medications. No family history of liver disease. The jaundice worsens with fasting or illness. She has had similar episodes since childhood that resolved on their own.
hints.pe: Mild scleral icterus today. No hepatomegaly. No splenomegaly. No spider angiomas. No asterixis. Abdomen soft and non-tender.
hints.img: RUQ Ultrasound: Normal liver echogenicity. No biliary dilation. No gallstones.

lab_results.CMP: Total bilirubin 4.2 — predominantly unconjugated. Direct bilirubin 0.3. ALT/AST/ALP all normal. Albumin normal.
lab_results.Specialty: UGT1A1 genotyping: Homozygous TA7 repeat (7/7) — Gilbert syndrome confirmed.

hx_brief: 49yo male with 3 weeks of progressive exertional dyspnea, orthopnea, and bilateral lower extremity swelling. He is an alcoholic.

hints.hx: He drinks a fifth of vodka daily for 15 years. No prior cardiac history. No cocaine use. No hypertension. He cannot lie flat without becoming short of breath.
hints.pe: HR 116 irregularly irregular. BP 96/64. JVP elevated. Bilateral crackles. S3 gallop. 4+ pitting edema to the thighs. Hepatojugular reflux positive.
hints.img: Echocardiogram: EF 18%. Four-chamber dilation. Moderate-to-severe mitral and tricuspid regurgitation. No wall motion abnormalities.

lab_results.EKG: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response.
lab_results.Specialty: BNP: 4,100 pg/mL. Thiamine level: Undetectable.

hx_brief: 24yo female with 3 days of sudden vision loss in her left eye. She describes it as a gray curtain descending from the top of her visual field.

hints.hx: She is highly myopic (-9.00 diopters). No trauma. No prior eye problems. No systemic symptoms. She noticed flashing lights and floaters 2 days before the vision loss started.
hints.pe: Visual acuity: Left eye — hand motion only. Right eye — 20/20. Left eye fundoscopy: Elevated billowing retinal surface, loss of choroidal detail, retinal folds visible.
hints.img: Ocular ultrasound B-scan: Highly echogenic membrane detached from the posterior pole, anchored at the optic disc.

lab_results.Specialty: Fluorescein angiography: Large superotemporal retinal detachment involving the macula. Horseshoe tear identified at 11 o'clock.

hx_brief: 55yo male with 4 months of worsening fatigue, bone pain in his back and hips, and recurrent infections. His primary care doctor found elevated calcium on routine labs.

hints.hx: He has had 3 pneumonias in the past year. He reports back pain that wakes him at night. He has lost 16 pounds. No lymphadenopathy noticed. No night sweats.
hints.pe: Pale and cachectic. Diffuse bony tenderness to palpation over the spine and sternum. No hepatosplenomegaly. No peripheral lymphadenopathy.
hints.img: Whole body MRI: Diffuse bone marrow infiltration. Multiple focal lesions in the spine, pelvis, and ribs.

lab_results.Specialty: Bone marrow: 60% plasma cells. FISH: del(17p) and t(4;14) — high-risk cytogenetics. IgA lambda M-spike on SPEP.

hx_brief: 40yo female with sudden onset severe chest pain and shortness of breath 4 days after returning from a 14-hour flight from Australia.

hints.hx: She sat in a window seat and barely moved during the flight. She is on oral contraceptives. No prior clots. No family history of clotting disorders. Left leg has been swollen since the flight.
hints.pe: HR 118. BP 100/72. O2 sat 88% on room air. Left leg swollen with calf tenderness. JVP elevated. Right heart strain signs on exam.
hints.img: CT Pulmonary Angiography: Bilateral saddle pulmonary embolism with thrombus in the main pulmonary artery extending into bilateral main branches.

lab_results.EKG: Sinus tachycardia. S1Q3T3 pattern. New right bundle branch block.
lab_results.ABG: pH 7.48. pO2 54. pCO2 28 — respiratory alkalosis with hypoxia.
lab_results.Specialty: Echo: RV dilation and hypokinesis. McConnell sign present. Estimated RVSP 58 mmHg.

hx_brief: 16yo male brought in by his mother after she noticed his urine was dark and he was urinating much less than usual. He had a sore throat 2 weeks ago.

hints.hx: He had an exudative pharyngitis that was treated with antibiotics — but he never completed the course. The dark urine started 3 days ago. He has puffiness around his eyes in the morning.
hints.pe: BP 148/92. Bilateral periorbital edema. 1+ pitting edema in bilateral lower extremities. No rash. Temperature normal.
hints.img: Renal Ultrasound: Bilaterally enlarged kidneys with increased echogenicity. No hydronephrosis.

lab_results.UA: Cola-colored urine. 3+ blood. 2+ protein. RBC casts on microscopy.
lab_results.Specialty: ASO titer: 1:1280 (markedly elevated). C3: Low. C4: Normal. Anti-DNase B: Positive.

hx_brief: 62yo male with 2 months of progressive difficulty swallowing, regurgitation of undigested food, and halitosis. He has lost 14 pounds.

hints.hx: The regurgitation happens hours after eating and the food is undigested — not acidic. He gets relief by pressing on his neck. He never has heartburn. Symptoms are worse at night.
hints.pe: Mild halitosis. A soft compressible swelling is visible in the left posterior neck that gurgles on palpation. No lymphadenopathy. No neck masses.
hints.img: Barium swallow: Posterior outpouching at the pharyngoesophageal junction filling with contrast — located just above the cricopharyngeus muscle.

lab_results.Specialty: Manometry: Incomplete relaxation and incoordination of the cricopharyngeus — increased hypopharyngeal pressure.

hx_brief: 32yo male with 3 days of severe watery diarrhea after finishing a course of clindamycin for a dental abscess. He has had up to 15 watery stools per day.

hints.hx: He completed a 10-day course of clindamycin 5 days ago. The diarrhea started on day 7 of the antibiotics. He has lower abdominal cramping and low-grade fever. No blood in stool.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.3°C. HR 102. Diffuse mild abdominal tenderness most prominent in the left lower quadrant. No peritoneal signs. Abdomen distended.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Diffuse colonic wall thickening with thumbprinting pattern. Pericolonic fat stranding. No free air.

lab_results.Specialty: C. difficile toxin PCR: Positive. GDH antigen: Positive. Toxin A/B EIA: Positive.

hx_brief: 58yo female with 6 months of progressive fatigue, jaundice, and severe itching that is worse at night. She has no history of alcohol use.

hints.hx: The itching started before the jaundice. She also has dry eyes and dry mouth. No prior liver disease. No hepatitis exposures. No medications. She has noticed dark urine and pale stools.
hints.pe: Mild scleral icterus. Xanthomas on eyelids. Excoriations from scratching. Hepatomegaly. No splenomegaly. No spider angiomas.
hints.img: MRCP: No biliary dilation. No stones. Normal bile duct caliber throughout.

lab_results.CMP: ALP 620 (markedly elevated). GGT elevated. Bilirubin 4.8. AST/ALT mildly elevated. Albumin 3.2.
lab_results.Specialty: AMA (anti-mitochondrial antibody): Positive M2 titer 1:640. IgM elevated. Liver biopsy: Florid bile duct lesion — lymphocytic infiltrate.

hx_brief: 45yo male with sudden onset severe lower back pain radiating to both legs. He also noticed he couldn't fully empty his bladder this morning.

hints.hx: No trauma. He had a lumbar disc herniation 3 years ago treated conservatively. The pain is different this time — more severe and bilateral. He has had difficulty starting urination for 2 days.
hints.pe: Decreased perianal sensation on pinprick. Reduced anal sphincter tone. Bilateral lower extremity weakness 3/5. Saddle anesthesia present. Bladder percussed to the umbilicus.
hints.img: MRI Lumbar Spine emergent: Large L4-L5 central disc herniation causing severe compression of the cauda equina nerve roots.

lab_results.Specialty: Post-void residual: 650mL (severely elevated). Urodynamics: Neurogenic bladder pattern.

hx_brief: 21yo female with 5 days of painful urination, vaginal discharge, and lower abdominal pain. She is sexually active.

hints.hx: New sexual partner 3 weeks ago. She uses condoms inconsistently. No prior STIs. She has had intermittent fever. The lower abdominal pain is bilateral and worsens with movement.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.1°C. Cervical motion tenderness on bimanual exam. Bilateral adnexal tenderness — right worse than left. Mucopurulent cervical discharge. No peritoneal signs.
hints.img: Pelvic Ultrasound: Thickened, fluid-filled fallopian tubes bilaterally. Small amount of free pelvic fluid.

lab_results.Specialty: NAAT: Chlamydia trachomatis positive. Neisseria gonorrhoeae negative.

hx_brief: 67yo male with 4 months of progressive exertional dyspnea and a harsh systolic murmur noted by his primary care doctor. He has had two syncopal episodes.

hints.hx: Syncopal episodes occurred during exertion. He also has exertional chest pain that resolves with rest. History of a bicuspid aortic valve noted incidentally 20 years ago. Never followed up.
hints.pe: BP 110/80 with narrow pulse pressure. Harsh crescendo-decrescendo systolic murmur at right upper sternal border radiating to carotids. Pulsus parvus et tardus. Diminished S2.
hints.img: Echocardiogram: Bicuspid aortic valve with severe calcification. Peak gradient 88 mmHg. Mean gradient 52 mmHg. AVA 0.7 cm².

lab_results.EKG: LVH with strain pattern. Left axis deviation.
lab_results.Specialty: Cath: Confirmed severe AS. EF 45% (mildly reduced). No significant CAD.

hx_brief: 36yo male with 6 weeks of progressive bilateral hand and wrist pain, morning stiffness lasting 2 hours, and extreme fatigue. He has developed a salmon-colored rash that comes and goes.

hints.hx: The rash appears with fever spikes and fades when the fever breaks. He has had daily fevers to 39.5°C for 6 weeks. He has lost 10 pounds. Multiple joints are involved — symmetric.
hints.pe: Quotidian salmon-colored evanescent rash on trunk and arms. Splenomegaly. Lymphadenopathy in multiple regions. Symmetric synovitis of wrists, MCPs, and knees.
hints.img: CT Chest/Abdomen: Mediastinal lymphadenopathy and splenomegaly. No masses.

lab_results.Specialty: Ferritin: 48,000 ng/mL (markedly elevated — >5x ULN). RF: Negative. ANA: Negative. Anti-CCP: Negative.

hx_brief: 52yo male with 3 months of episodic severe pain in his right great toe. The attacks come on suddenly, usually at night, and the toe becomes red, swollen, and unbearable to touch.

hints.hx: He drinks 4-5 beers nightly. He recently started hydrochlorothiazide for hypertension. He has had 3 prior episodes. He eats red meat and shellfish frequently.
hints.pe: Right first MTP joint is erythematous, warm, swollen, and exquisitely tender to light touch. Tophi visible on the right ear helix and left Achilles tendon. No other joint involvement.
hints.img: X-ray right foot: Soft tissue swelling at first MTP. Periarticular erosions with overhanging edges — rat-bite appearance.

lab_results.Specialty: Joint fluid: Negatively birefringent needle-shaped monosodium urate crystals. WBC 42,000 — predominantly neutrophils.

hx_brief: 29yo female with 3 months of progressive proximal muscle weakness, elevated liver enzymes, and an unusual rash on her knuckles.

hints.hx: She works outdoors and thought the rash was from sun exposure. The weakness makes it difficult to climb stairs or raise her arms. No joint pain. No prior autoimmune history.
hints.pe: Scaly, erythematous papules over the MCP and PIP joints — Gottron papules. V-sign rash on anterior chest. Proximal muscle strength 2/5 upper and lower extremities. No heliotrope rash.
hints.img: MRI Thighs: Diffuse muscle edema and inflammatory changes.

lab_results.Specialty: CK: 12,400 U/L. Anti-MDA5: Positive. HRCT Chest: Interstitial lung disease — organizing pneumonia pattern.

hx_brief: 44yo male presents with severe epigastric pain, nausea, and diarrhea. His symptoms have recurred despite multiple courses of PPI therapy over 2 years.

hints.hx: He has had 4 peptic ulcers in 2 years — all in unusual locations, including the distal duodenum and jejunum. PPI therapy temporarily helps but symptoms always return. He has also had diarrhea for months.
hints.pe: Mild epigastric tenderness. No peritoneal signs. No palpable masses. Normal bowel sounds.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Thickening of duodenal and jejunal folds. No discrete mass identified. Prominent gastric folds.

lab_results.Specialty: Fasting serum gastrin: 1,840 pg/mL (normal <100). Secretin stimulation test: Gastrin rises >200 pg/mL — paradoxical increase. Somatostatin receptor scintigraphy: Pancreatic head lesion.

hx_brief: 38yo male with sudden onset of severe tearing chest pain that woke him from sleep. He is 6'4" and very thin. He plays basketball recreationally.

hints.hx: He has had intermittent joint hypermobility and his lens dislocated once in his left eye as a teenager. He wears very thick glasses. His father died of a heart problem at age 42.
hints.pe: Tall, thin habitus. Arm span exceeds height. High-arched palate. Pectus excavatum. Murmur of aortic regurgitation at left sternal border. BP differential between arms.
hints.img: CTA Chest: Type A aortic dissection with intimal flap extending from the aortic root. Aortic root diameter 6.2cm.

lab_results.EKG: LVH pattern. No acute ST changes.
lab_results.Specialty: FBN1 mutation: Pathogenic variant identified — confirms Marfan syndrome.

hx_brief: 55yo male with 3 months of progressive shortness of breath, dry cough, and bilateral ankle swelling. He worked in shipbuilding for 30 years.

hints.hx: He was exposed to asbestos daily for decades without adequate protection. Non-smoker. No prior lung disease. The dyspnea has been worsening steadily — no acute episodes.
hints.pe: Decreased breath sounds at bilateral bases with dullness to percussion. No crackles. Mild clubbing. No lymphadenopathy.
hints.img: CT Chest: Large bilateral pleural effusions. Pleural thickening and calcified pleural plaques. Pleural-based irregular soft tissue masses.

lab_results.Specialty: Pleural fluid: Exudative. Cytology: Malignant mesothelial cells. Biopsy: Epithelioid mesothelioma. Calretinin and WT-1 positive.

hx_brief: 22yo male with 3 days of fever, confusion, and bizarre behavior. His family says he has been acting like a different person — aggressive and then withdrawn.

hints.hx: He had a mild cold-like illness 2 weeks ago. No prior psychiatric history. No drug use confirmed. He had one generalized seizure at home. He has been hallucinating and hypersexual.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.6°C. Confused and agitated — MMSE 14/30. Abnormal involuntary mouth movements. Autonomic instability — BP fluctuating. No focal deficits.
hints.img: MRI Brain FLAIR: Bilateral medial temporal lobe T2 signal hyperintensity. No mass effect.

lab_results.Specialty: CSF: Anti-NMDA receptor antibodies: Strongly positive. Lymphocytic pleocytosis. CT Abdomen: No teratoma identified.

hx_brief: 48yo male with 6 weeks of fatigue, weight loss, and a persistent cough. A chest X-ray at his primary care doctor showed an abnormality.

hints.hx: He lived in the Ohio River Valley for 20 years before moving. Immunocompetent. Non-smoker. Works as a construction worker doing soil excavation. No sick contacts.
hints.pe: Low-grade fever 37.9°C. Mild hepatomegaly. Cervical lymphadenopathy. Fine crackles at right base. No skin lesions.
hints.img: CT Chest: Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules and a right hilar mass. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy. CT Abdomen: Hepatic nodules.

lab_results.Specialty: Urine Histoplasma antigen: Positive (high titer). Serum antigen: Positive. BAL culture: Histoplasma capsulatum.

hx_brief: 31yo female with 2 months of recurrent abdominal pain, bloating, and alternating constipation and diarrhea. She rates the pain 6/10 and it improves after bowel movements.

hints.hx: Symptoms began during a particularly stressful period at work. She has had extensive workup previously — all normal. No blood in stool. No nocturnal symptoms. No weight loss. No family history of IBD or colon cancer.
hints.pe: Mild diffuse abdominal tenderness without guarding or rigidity. No organomegaly. Normal bowel sounds. Rectal exam normal.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Completely normal. Colonoscopy: Normal mucosa throughout. Biopsies: Normal colonic mucosa.

lab_results.Specialty: Fecal calprotectin: 28 mcg/g (normal <50 — no mucosal inflammation). Fecal lactoferrin: Negative. Rome IV criteria met.

hx_brief: 59yo male with 4 months of progressive bilateral leg weakness, muscle fasciculations, and difficulty swallowing. He has also noticed his speech sounds more nasal.

hints.hx: No family history of neuromuscular disease. Non-smoker. No toxic exposures. He has noticed muscle twitching throughout his body. His grip strength has declined significantly.
hints.pe: Fasciculations visible in bilateral thighs and upper arms. Tongue fasciculations. Hyperreflexia in all four extremities. Bilateral Babinski signs. Dysarthria. No sensory deficits.
hints.img: MRI Brain and Spine: No compressive lesion. Mild corticospinal tract T2 signal in bilateral internal capsules.

lab_results.Specialty: EMG: Active denervation (fibrillations, positive sharp waves) and chronic reinnervation in 3 body regions. NCS: Normal sensory conductions. Confirms upper and lower motor neuron involvement.

hx_brief: 26yo female with 2 weeks of fatigue, fever, severe sore throat, and swollen glands. She kissed someone at a party 3 weeks ago who later said he was ill.

hints.hx: The fatigue is debilitating — she cannot get out of bed. She had a brief rash after taking amoxicillin for what was thought to be strep throat. No prior similar episodes.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.8°C. Marked bilateral posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Tonsillar exudates. Splenomegaly — palpable 4cm below costal margin. Maculopapular rash on trunk.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Splenomegaly 16cm. No splenic laceration.

lab_results.CBC: Lymphocytosis — 68% lymphocytes with >10% atypical lymphocytes. Mild thrombocytopenia.
lab_results.Specialty: Monospot: Positive. EBV VCA IgM: Positive. EBV EA: Positive. EBNA: Negative — confirms acute primary infection.
